proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_012 is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear relationship across the full cohort).

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df.feature_012, df.pfs_months); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_012', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=0.702 (p=0.00e+00); per-unit slope from OLS = +0.1759 months per unit (p=0.00e+00). feature_012 is the strongest single predictor of pfs_months in the cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months decreases monotonically across the three ordered levels of feature_005 (level 0 highest, level 2 lowest).

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway(*[df.loc[df.feature_005==v,'pfs_months'] for v in [0,1,2]])
analyses.result_summary: Means by level: 0=5.64, 1=4.44, 2=3.29 months. ANOVA F p=0.00e+00. Level 2 vs level 0 = -2.35 months. Monotonic decrease confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_006=1 have shorter pfs_months than those with feature_006=0 (adverse prognostic marker, ~30% prevalence).
Patients with feature_001=1 have shorter pfs_months than those with feature_001=0 (adverse marker, ~10% prevalence).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(feature_006) + feature_012 + C(feature_005) + feature_004 + feature_024 + feature_033 + C(feature_001)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_006=1 effect = -1.551 months (p=0.00e+00). Strongly negative.

analyses.code: same multivariable model — read C(feature_001)[T.1] coefficient
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_001=1 effect = -0.993 months (p=0.00e+00). Strongly negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013=1 is associated with longer pfs_months (favorable factor; candidate treatment indicator, ~35% prevalence).
feature_029=1 is associated with longer pfs_months (favorable factor, ~70% prevalence).
feature_023=1 is associated with longer pfs_months (favorable factor, ~64% prevalence).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 + feature_012 + C(feature_005) + C(feature_006) + C(feature_001) + feature_004 + feature_024 + feature_033', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_013=1 adjusted effect = +1.102 months (p=0.00e+00). Strongly positive.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_029 + feature_012 + C(feature_005) + C(feature_006) + C(feature_001) + feature_004 + feature_024 + feature_033', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_029=1 adjusted effect = +0.547 months (p=0.00e+00).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_023 + feature_012 + C(feature_005) + C(feature_006) + C(feature_001) + feature_004 + feature_024 + feature_033', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_023=1 adjusted effect = +0.359 months (p=2.53e-189).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_028=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months (adverse factor, ~35% prevalence).
feature_018=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months (adverse factor, ~18% prevalence).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_028 + feature_012 + C(feature_005) + C(feature_006) + C(feature_001) + feature_004 + feature_024 + feature_033', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_028=1 adjusted effect = -0.588 months (p=0.00e+00). Negative — possibly a confounded treatment indicator or a true adverse marker.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_018 + feature_012 + C(feature_005) + C(feature_006) + C(feature_001) + feature_004 + feature_024 + feature_033', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_018=1 adjusted effect = -0.468 months (p=1.66e-206). Negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_024 is associated with longer pfs_months (positive continuous predictor).
Higher feature_004 is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative continuous predictor).
Higher feature_033 is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative continuous predictor).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_024 + ...', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_024 adjusted slope = +0.4845 months per unit (p=0.00e+00).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_004 + ...', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_004 adjusted slope = -0.0770 months per unit (p=0.00e+00).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_033 + ...', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_033 adjusted slope = -0.0188 months per unit (p=0.00e+00).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 (a balanced binary, ~45% prevalence) has no detectable association with pfs_months.
feature_031 has a small but non-zero negative association with pfs_months after adjustment.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(feature_011) + feature_012 + C(feature_005) + C(feature_006) + C(feature_001) + feature_004 + feature_024 + feature_033', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_011=1 adjusted effect = +0.0141 (p=0.235). Null result.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_031 + feature_012 + C(feature_005) + C(feature_006) + C(feature_001) + feature_004 + feature_024 + feature_033', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_031 adjusted slope = -0.00054 (p=9.86e-14). Tiny but significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Together, the 37 features explain a large share (>=70%) of variance in pfs_months in a single linear model.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ ' + all-features, df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Full OLS R^2 = 0.792; F-statistic p≈0. Confirms most of the variation in pfs_months is captured by these features collectively.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The strong binary predictors (feature_006, feature_001, feature_013, feature_028, feature_029, feature_023, feature_018, feature_016) are essentially uncorrelated with each other (max |r| < 0.4), so none is acting as a proxy for another.

analyses.code: df[strong_bin].corr().abs() ; np.fill_diagonal(.., 0); max()
analyses.result_summary: Maximum absolute Pearson correlation among strong binary predictors = 0.669. The single largest pairwise dependency is feature_018 vs feature_016 (~ -0.36); all others are <0.02. Consistent with these features being approximately independent in the cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 has heterogeneous effect on pfs_months across multiple other features (i.e., shows >=3 pairwise treatment-by-feature interactions at p<1e-10), consistent with being a treatment whose efficacy depends on biomarkers.
feature_006 and feature_001 have NO strong (p<1e-10) interactions with any other feature, consistent with being pure prognostic biomarkers rather than treatments.

analyses.code: loop pairwise interactions feature_013*X over all binary X
analyses.result_summary: feature_013 has 5 pairwise binary interactions with p<1e-10 (top 5 partners by p-value: feature_028, feature_029, feature_018, feature_023, feature_016). Strong evidence of treatment-effect modification.

analyses.code: same loop for feature_006 and feature_001
analyses.result_summary: feature_006: 0 strong binary interactions; feature_001: 0 strong binary interactions. Both behave as pure prognostic markers (no detectable effect modifiers). For comparison, feature_028 has 4 strong interactions (treatment-like).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favorable effect of feature_013 on pfs_months is markedly larger when feature_029=1 than when feature_029=0 (positive interaction).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 * C(feature_029)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_013 main = ~0 when feature_029=0; interaction feature_013:feature_029=1 coefficient = +1.594 (p=9.31e-229). Treatment effect of feature_013 in feature_029=0 stratum is essentially null; in feature_029=1 it is +1.6 mo (unadjusted).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favorable effect of feature_013 is suppressed (interaction is negative) when feature_018=1.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 * C(feature_018)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_013:feature_018=1 coefficient = -1.364 (p=2.19e-118). When feature_018=1 the feature_013 benefit collapses to ~0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favorable effect of feature_013 is suppressed when feature_028=1 (negative interaction).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 * C(feature_028)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_013:feature_028=1 coefficient = -1.623 (p=4.00e-255). When feature_028=1 the feature_013 benefit collapses to ~0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_033 attenuates the favorable effect of feature_013 on pfs_months (negative continuous interaction).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 * feature_033 + C(feature_029) + C(feature_018) + C(feature_028)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_013:feature_033 coefficient = -0.05229 (p=7.59e-179). Treatment benefit declines as feature_033 increases.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The prognostic biomarkers feature_006, feature_001, and feature_005 do NOT modify the treatment effect of feature_013 (interaction p-values not significant).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 * C(feature_006)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_013:feature_006 interaction = +0.006 (p=0.90); feature_013:feature_001 = -0.041 (p=0.59); feature_013:feature_005[T.2] = +0.035 (p=0.60). All non-significant — these are pure prognostic, not predictive, biomarkers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the joint subgroup defined by feature_029=1 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0, the feature_013 treatment effect on pfs_months is large (>2 months) and highly significant.
Outside that joint subgroup (any one of feature_029=0, feature_018=1, or feature_028=1), feature_013 has no detectable effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 + ...', df[mask3]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: In subgroup (n=18681): feature_013 adjusted effect = +2.921 months (p=0.00e+00).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 + ...', df[~mask3]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Outside subgroup (n=31319): feature_013 adjusted effect = +0.0005 months (p=0.84). Null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the 3-condition subgroup, there is a sharp feature_033 threshold around 14: below it the feature_013 benefit is large (~+5 months); above it the benefit collapses to ~0.

analyses.code: for thr in [10,12,13,14,15,16,18,20,25]: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013', df[mask3 & (df.feature_033<=thr)]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Threshold scan: thr=10: n=7362, eff=+4.96, p=0.0e+00; thr=12: n=9206, eff=+4.95, p=0.0e+00; thr=13: n=10036, eff=+4.92, p=0.0e+00; thr=14: n=10828, eff=+4.94, p=0.0e+00; thr=15: n=11575, eff=+4.65, p=0.0e+00; thr=16: n=12203, eff=+4.42, p=0.0e+00; thr=18: n=13326, eff=+4.09, p=0.0e+00; thr=20: n=14226, eff=+3.83, p=0.0e+00; thr=25: n=15963, eff=+3.39, p=0.0e+00. The effect estimate is stable around +5 mo for thresholds <=14 and decays sharply for thresholds >=15, indicating a discrete cutoff near feature_033 ≈ 14.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the 4-condition subgroup feature_029=1 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_033<=14, feature_013 increases pfs_months by approximately 5 months (adjusted).
Outside this 4-condition subgroup, feature_013 has zero effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 + ...', df[mask4]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: In refined subgroup (n=10828): feature_013 adjusted effect = +4.977 months (p=0.00e+00).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 + ...', df[~mask4]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Outside refined subgroup (n=39172): feature_013 adjusted effect = -0.0007 months (p=0.74). Effectively zero.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the 4-condition refined subgroup, no further feature (binary or continuous) significantly modifies the feature_013 treatment effect — i.e., the subgroup definition is complete.

analyses.code: loop interactions feature_013*X over all features within df[mask4], threshold p<1e-3
analyses.result_summary: Within the refined subgroup (n=10828): 0 additional binary effect modifiers and 0 additional continuous modifiers found at p<1e-3. The subgroup definition {feature_029=1, feature_018=0, feature_028=0, feature_033<=14} is complete; no remaining residual heterogeneity in feature_013 effect within it.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A 4-way model of feature_013 × feature_029 × feature_018 × feature_028 reveals the entire treatment-effect heterogeneity sits in the cells where feature_029=1 and feature_018=0 and feature_028=0, with significant 3-way interactions cancelling the effect when feature_018=1 or feature_028=1.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 * feature_029 * feature_018 * feature_028', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Key terms: feature_013:feature_029 = +2.950 (p=0.00e+00); feature_013:feature_029:feature_018 = -2.892 (p=3.42e-81); feature_013:feature_029:feature_028 = -3.007 (p=2.13e-165). Pattern: large +3 mo benefit appears only when feature_029 turns on, then cancels via two negative 3-way terms when feature_018 or feature_028 also turn on.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The apparent harm of feature_028 (negative main effect on pfs_months) is mediated entirely by its abrogation of the feature_013 treatment benefit: among feature_013=0 patients, feature_028 has no effect; among feature_013=1 patients (within the eligible subgroup), feature_028=1 substantially reduces pfs_months.

analyses.code: stratify by feature_013 within feature_018=0 & feature_029=1
analyses.result_summary: feature_013=0 stratum: feature_028 effect = +0.034 (p=0.29). feature_013=1 stratum: feature_028 effect = -2.852 (p=0.00e+00). Confirms feature_028's apparent harm is a treatment-effect-erasure phenomenon, not its own outcome effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Like feature_028, the apparent harm of feature_018 is driven by its erasure of the feature_013 effect: within feature_013=0 patients feature_018 has near-zero effect; within feature_013=1 patients feature_018=1 sharply lowers pfs_months.

analyses.code: stratify by feature_013 within feature_028=0 & feature_029=1
analyses.result_summary: feature_013=0 stratum: feature_018 effect = +0.023 (p=0.62). feature_013=1 stratum: feature_018 effect = -2.902 (p=3.62e-221). Same pattern as feature_028 — feature_018=1 marks resistance to feature_013.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 and feature_016 do NOT need to be added to the subgroup definition — within the 4-condition refined subgroup, feature_013 effect is essentially identical at all values of these features.

analyses.code: stratify df[mask4] by feature_023 and by feature_016
analyses.result_summary: feature_023=0: eff=+4.627 (p=7.2e-242); feature_023=1: eff=+4.997 (p=0.0e+00). feature_016=0: eff=+4.878 (p=0.0e+00); feature_016=1: eff=+5.022 (p=0.0e+00). Effect ~+5 mo across all four cells; no further refinement justified.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A fully saturated interaction model among the four modifiers reproduces the same result: predicted feature_013 contrast is large (~+5 mo) inside the subgroup and ~0 outside.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 * feature_029 * feature_018 * feature_028 * feature_033', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Predicted feature_013 contrast at one in-subgroup row = +3.251 mo; at one out-subgroup row = +0.091 mo. Saturated model is consistent with simpler stratified estimates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final treatment-effect-heterogeneity hypothesis (best supported by the data): feature_013 increases pfs_months ONLY in patients meeting all four conditions: feature_029=1 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_033<=14. Within this subgroup the pfs_months difference is approximately +5 months (treated vs untreated). In every patient who fails any one of these conditions the effect is ≈0. feature_006, feature_001, feature_005 (and other prognostic features) DO predict pfs_months but do NOT modify the feature_013 treatment effect.

analyses.code: df[mask4].groupby('feature_013')['pfs_months'].mean(); df[~mask4].groupby('feature_013')['pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: In refined subgroup (n=10828): treated mean pfs_months = 9.228, untreated = 4.285, unadjusted difference = +4.943 months (matches adjusted ~+5.0). Outside subgroup (n=39172): treated mean = 4.324, untreated = 4.317, unadjusted difference = +0.007 months (≈0). Final supported hypothesis: feature_013's PFS benefit is fully concentrated in the subgroup feature_029=1 ∧ feature_018=0 ∧ feature_028=0 ∧ feature_033≤14.
